text:
Well, there once was a curious little hedgehog. Every morning, he embarked on an adventure to explore a colorful garden full of butterflies. One day, he came across a peculiar object—an oversized book lying open on a sunbeam. Fascinated, he picked it up. It was a historical encyclopedia about animals! Hours flew by while he read about various creatures...

Once done reading, the hedgehog realized the moon was big. It was bigger than any star. That night, he dreamed about stars. They were all so tiny...

When dawn arrived, full-circle again, the hedgehog jumped into his world-saving adventure!

With newfound star wisdom, he returned the big moonshade to its rightful place. The birds love stars!

And so our hero returned home, with his star charm gleaming. Tales like these linger longest in sleepy burrows, and our dear hedgehog became the star-know-your-details champ.

That’s why folks say little things like “That’s really a star?”

Just another
